post.content:
I am looking for a solution and the possible approaches for the following question

You are given a rectangular grid which you want to paint using different colors, but since you are not an experienced painter, you have certain restrictions with your art:
1. On each brush stroke, you’ll paint a solid rectangular pattern of a single colour on the grid, This will cover up the existing colours in that rectangular area.
2. Once you have used a certain colour, you won’t use that colour again.

You are given a ‘m x n’ matrix ‘targetGrid’, where ‘targetGrid[row][col’ is the colour in the position (row, col) of the grid. Return true if it is possible to paint the matrix targetGrid, otherwise, return false.

1. [ [1,1,1,1], [1,1,3,3], [1,1,2,3], [5,5,3,4] ] Ans: True
2. [ [1,2,1], [2,1,2], [1,1,2], ] Ans: False
